ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general coffee beverage class; no specific Vietnamese black iced coffee term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers black coffee generically; no specific Vietnamese cold/iced variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34718003 refers to coffee as a substance; no specific Vietnamese iced black coffee concept exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 ml (200 g) of cold Vietnamese black coffee served over ice, without sugar syrup unless optionally added.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14209)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Vietnamese black coffee (cold/iced, no sugar) is nutritionally equivalent to brewed black coffee per 100 g of liquid. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for brewed black coffee (NDB 14209 / FDC ID 171890) used as the reference. Values reflect unsweetened black coffee only; optional sugar syrup is excluded per the input description.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Black Coffee — effectively zero glycemic response; convention value assigned
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain black coffee contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g) and produces no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature consistently classify unsweetened black coffee as having a negligible or zero GI. Per Project Veritas convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (lowest valid positive value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Unsweetened Vietnamese black coffee contains only 2 mg sodium per 100 g and poses minimal hypertension risk from sodium; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine in coffee can transiently raise blood pressure and moderate daily intake to 1–2 cups is generally considered acceptable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no sugar in unsweetened form
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Vietnamese black coffee has a negligible glycemic index and zero carbohydrates, making it safe for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the optional sugar syrup (approximately 25.5 g sugar per 30 ml) should be avoided or strictly limited as it would significantly raise the glycemic load of the beverage.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; moderate caffeine intake associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Unsweetened Vietnamese black coffee contains virtually no fat or saturated fat and moderate habitual coffee consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) has been associated with neutral to modestly beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in epidemiological studies; individuals with arrhythmias or severe cardiovascular disease should consult their physician regarding caffeine intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vietnamese Black Coffee (Cold), known in Vietnamese as Cà Phê Đen Đá (Ca Phe Den Da), is a traditional Vietnamese beverage prepared by slowly drip-brewing dark-roasted Vietnamese robusta coffee through a small metal phin filter directly over a glass of ice. The result is a strong, intensely flavoured, slightly bitter black coffee served cold. Vietnamese robusta beans are characteristically bold and earthy with a higher caffeine content than arabica varieties. The beverage is consumed widely across Vietnam and in Vietnamese diaspora communities, including in Singapore where it is served at Vietnamese restaurants. It is typically offered with optional sugar syrup on the side, allowing the drinker to adjust sweetness. Without sugar syrup, the drink is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 g), contains negligible protein (0.12 g), negligible fat (0.02 g), and zero carbohydrates, making it suitable for low-calorie, low-carbohydrate, and diabetic-friendly diets. It is naturally vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free. Clinically, the unsweetened form poses minimal concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, though the optional sugar syrup adds approximately 25.5 g of sugar per 30 ml serving and should be avoided by individuals managing blood glucose. The beverage is rich in potassium (49 mg/100 g) and riboflavin (0.18 mg/100 g) relative to its caloric content.

confidence_reason: Nutritional values for unsweetened brewed black coffee are well-established in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171890). Vietnamese black coffee (cold/iced, no sugar) is nutritionally equivalent to standard brewed black coffee per 100 g of liquid. The GI is effectively zero and well-supported by the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature on coffee. Multilingual Vietnamese naming is confirmed. Confidence is high.
notes: This record covers Vietnamese Black Coffee (Cold) in its unsweetened form only. The optional sugar syrup described in the input (30 ml ≈ 25.5 g sugar) is excluded from all nutrient calculations. Consumers who add sugar syrup will significantly increase energy and carbohydrate content. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 g, reflecting the negligible but non-zero caloric content of brewed black coffee per USDA SR Legacy. protein_g (0.12 g) and fat_g (0.02 g) are naturally present in trace amounts in brewed coffee and are not zero; carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — confirmed zero. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. monounsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171890 and set to reported values. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as USDA SR Legacy reports all listed nutrients for brewed black coffee. Cuisine is classified as Vietnamese (origin) though the input listed Singapore as the delivery market context.

Documented all zero macro values in notes with confirmed zero justification phrases.
